# Sachin Sarwa — schnsrw.live

> Portfolio for three open-source, file-centric productivity tools:
> Casual Sheets (Excel-flavored web spreadsheet), Casual Editor
> (Word-flavored web document editor), and Casual Desktop (Tauri
> binaries — in progress). All three round-trip native Office formats
> with high fidelity and are designed to be self-hosted via Docker.

## About

- **Person:** Sachin Sarwa — software engineer
- **Contact:** schnsrw@gmail.com
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/schnsrw
- **Portfolio:** https://schnsrw.live/

## Projects

### Casual Sheets — Excel-flavored web spreadsheet
- **Live demo:** https://sheet.schnsrw.live/
- **Source:** https://github.com/schnsrw/sheets
- **Docker:** https://hub.docker.com/r/schnsrw/casual-sheets
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Status:** v0.0.6 shipping. 357 Playwright end-to-end tests on every push.
- **Formats round-tripped:** .xlsx · .ods · .csv · .tsv
- **Built on:** Univer OSS (Apache-2.0) with a custom Office-style shell.
- **Notable features:** 8 chart types with trendlines + date-axis detection;
  pivot tables with drill-down; sparklines (line / column / win-loss); version
  history with auto-snapshots; conditional formatting; data validation; live
  cursors and presence avatars in the Docker image; .xlsm macro passthrough
  (round-trips byte-equal); 11-template home gallery with thumbnail previews.

### Casual Editor — Word-flavored web document editor
- **Live demo:** https://doc.schnsrw.live/
- **Source:** https://github.com/schnsrw/docx
- **License:** Apache-2.0 (fork of eigenpal/docx-editor, MIT)
- **Status:** Public preview. 26 of 39 .docx fixtures round-trip pristine in
  the per-tag audit; working toward ≥ 90% before the desktop ship.
- **Formats round-tripped:** .docx
- **Built on:** ProseMirror with an OOXML-preserving model; React + Bun; Go
  for the stateless real-time gateway (own y-websocket protocol implementation
  in ~120 LOC, no DB, no on-disk update log).
- **Notable features:** 14-template home gallery with real first-page PNG
  previews across four categories (Personal / Work / Education / Career);
  three-way fidelity harness vs LibreOffice and OnlyOffice DocumentBuilder;
  per-tag round-trip audit in CI; Word-compat opt-in flag for non-spec
  heuristics (e.g. last-row closing border, issue #395); pluggable host
  integration (inline / WOPI / JWT-API behind one Go interface); real-time
  co-editing via Yjs.

### Casual Desktop — offline editor for documents and sheets
- **Status:** Early Tauri scaffolding. First binary ships after both web
  fidelity scores cross the 90% floor.
- **Targets:** macOS, Linux, Windows.
- **Approach:** Reuses the Casual Editor + Casual Sheets web cores verbatim;
  deltas live in the Tauri shell. Single-user only (collab stays in the
  Docker images where it earns its keep).

## Architectural principles

- **File-centric.** Native Office formats are the source of truth. Open,
  edit, save back. Never an export-only model.
- **Stateless backends.** No DB, no on-disk update log in the real-time
  service. State lives only in memory while a session is active. The host
  owns persistence (WOPI in v1+, inline in v0).
- **Open source.** Apache-2.0 / MIT throughout. Demos free; self-host via
  the published Docker images.
- **Real-time when it earns its keep.** Single-user demo on GitHub Pages;
  real-time + co-edit ship in the Docker image, not in the React component
  itself. Same web build across distribution shapes.

## Tech stack

- **Frontend:** TypeScript · React · Vite · Bun · ProseMirror · Univer
- **Realtime + backend:** Go · Node.js · Fastify · Yjs · Hocuspocus ·
  Docker · Redis
- **Quality + ops:** Playwright · GitHub Actions · LibreOffice fidelity
  harness · ESLint + Prettier
- **Desktop:** Tauri · Rust
